channel.title: RoPE on Chen Kai Blog
channel.description: Recent content in RoPE on Chen Kai Blog

item.title: LLM Engineering (6): Long Context — RoPE, YaRN, Sinks
item.description: “1M token context” is one of the most over-claimed numbers in LLMs. A model can attend to 1M tokens — that’s an architecture statement. A model can use information at position 800K to answer a question — that’s a behavior statement, and it’s harder. This chapter is about the math of position encoding, the engineering tricks that extend context past the training length, and the reasons most long-context claims don’t survive needle-in-a-haystack tests.

item.title: NLP (9): Deep Dive into LLM Architecture
item.description: The 2017 Transformer paper drew one block. Every production LLM today still uses that diagram as a silhouette, but almost every internal piece has been replaced. Pre-norm replaced post-norm. RMSNorm replaced LayerNorm. SwiGLU replaced GELU. Rotary embeddings replaced sinusoids. Multi-head attention became grouped-query attention. The dense FFN sometimes became a sparse mixture of experts. And the inference loop is dominated by a data structure that doesn’t appear in the original paper at all: the KV cache.
